inline_comments.message:
Dave had these comments:

Will that create a unique file instance for each node? Just thinking you could have local shards, in such case you would want each node to have its own instance of the daexim directory.

There will be a separate persistent volume for each appc instance. Therefore effectively there will be a unique file per instance.
Didn't grasp the nodes and local shards in this context. do you mean k8s nodes or appc instances?
MD-SAL shards can be replicated or local, Dave wanted to make sure if you had a local shard that it was backed up and could be restored. I think we are good here based on your response.

There's an outstanding bug raised to make the default APP-C deployment have just a single instance (OOM-926).  Clustering will be enabled in a production version of the top-level values.yaml.
I suggest wrapping the creation of the 2nd and 3rd PVs in a condition that checks against the replicaCount for APP-C.  That way we'll avoid any unnecessary PVs.

I see the MDSAL data in a different parent directory structure than the APPC database data. Is there a reason to segment the data in different parent directory (/dockerdata-nfs/appc vs. /dockerdata-nfs/onap/appc) structures?

$ ls -la /dockerdata-nfs/appc/appc

drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr 19 06:55 mdsal1
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Apr 19 06:55 mdsal2

$ kubectl get pv
NAME                CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   RECLAIM POLICY   STATUS    CLAIM                             STORAGECLASS        REASON    AGE
appc-appc-data0     1Gi        RWO            Retain           Bound     onap/appc-appc-data-appc-appc-2   appc-appc-data                12s
appc-appc-data1     1Gi        RWO            Retain           Bound     onap/appc-appc-data-appc-appc-0   appc-appc-data                12s
appc-appc-data2     1Gi        RWO            Retain           Bound     onap/appc-appc-data-appc-appc-1   appc-appc-data                12s
appc-appc-db-data   1Gi        RWX            Retain           Bound     onap/appc-appc-db-data            appc-appc-db-data             12s
The folders do need to be separate, as they're owned by PVs from different charts, but should share a common parent.

I see a problem when I uninstall APPC with these new volumes. They do not clean up properly and I can not install APPC again.

$ helm install local/onap -n appc --namespace onap
Error: release appc failed: object is being deleted: persistentvolumes "appc-appc-data1" already exists
ubuntu@k8s-master:~/oom/kubernetes$ helm del --purge appc
release "appc" deleted
ubuntu@k8s-master:~/oom/kubernetes$ kubectl get pvc
No resources found.
ubuntu@k8s-master:~/oom/kubernetes$ kubectl get pv
NAME              CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   RECLAIM POLICY   STATUS        CLAIM                             STORAGECLASS     REASON    AGE
appc-appc-data0   1Gi        RWO            Retain           Terminating   onap/appc-appc-data-appc-appc-2   appc-appc-data             4d
appc-appc-data1   1Gi        RWO            Retain           Terminating   onap/appc-appc-data-appc-appc-0   appc-appc-data             4d
appc-appc-data2   1Gi        RWO            Retain           Terminating   onap/appc-appc-data-appc-appc-1   appc-appc-data             4d

Aaron, you can see in the error that the object is being deleted. You should let k8s finish cleanup of the deleted PVs and then try to redeploy appc.
I deployed appc, purged it and deployed again:
root@borislav-new-rancher:~/onap/master/appc/oom/kubernetes# kgpa

NAME               CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   RECLAIM POLICY   STATUS    CLAIM                           STORAGECLASS       REASON    AGE

NAME                       STATUS    VOLUME             CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   STORAGECLASS       AGE
aaa-appc-data-aaa-appc-0   Bound     aaa-appc-data1     1Gi        RWO            aaa-appc-data      2h
aaa-appc-data-aaa-appc-1   Bound     aaa-appc-data2     1Gi        RWO            aaa-appc-data      2h

aaa-appc-db-data           Bound     aaa-appc-db-data   1Gi        RWX            aaa-appc-db-data   5m

You can see the PVCs are 2h olds while the PVs are 5m old.
Should this be commented out?  I am confused by commenting out this piece but keeping the persistence.enabled values.yaml entry.

I don't see a definition for .Values.persistence.existingClaim.  If one were to define this and set it to true, what would the expected behaviour/use case be?  Is this for installing on top of an old deployment to (re-)use the MD-SAL DB?
In production usually the PVs will be created separately. This PV is basic, to make ONAP deploy OOB.

It is not commented. Helm still parses and works with this.
The '#'-s can be removed though everywhere...
Just curious, is it needed to support a cluster size of 5?

I do not know what size of a cluster maybe needed, however 5 looks like a nice number. I doubt the cluster will be larger.
APPC did test with 3.
